  | 0 => c
  | n++ => f n (recurse f c n)

-- Basic equations for recurse
@[simp] theorem recurse_zero (f : Nat → Nat → Nat) (c : Nat) : recurse f c 0 = c := rfl
@[simp] theorem recurse_succ (f : Nat → Nat → Nat) (c : Nat) (n : Nat) :
    recurse f c (n++) = f n (recurse f c n) := rfl

abbrev add (n m : Nat) : Nat :=
  recurse (fun _ sum ↦ sum++) m n

instance instAdd : Add Nat where
  add := add

-- Some basic lemmas about addition (from earlier sections)
theorem zero_succ : (0 : Nat)++ = 1 := rfl

theorem one_succ : (1 : Nat)++ = 2 := rfl

-- We include these as theorems; proofs are deferred (not definitions)
theorem add_zero (n : Nat) : n + 0 = n := by
  sorry

theorem zero_add (n : Nat) : 0 + n = n := by
  sorry

theorem add_succ (n m : Nat) : n + m++ = (n + m)++ := by
  sorry

theorem add_assoc (a b c : Nat) : (a + b) + c = a + (b + c) := by
  sorry

abbrev mul (n m : Nat) : Nat :=
  recurse (fun _ prod ↦ prod + m) 0 n

  mul := mul

/-- Definition 2.3.1 (Multiplication of natural numbers)
Compare with Mathlib's `Nat.zero_mul` -/
@[simp] theorem zero_mul (m: Nat) : 0 * m = 0 := recurse_zero (fun _ prod ↦ prod+m) _

/-- Definition 2.3.1 (Multiplication of natural numbers)
Compare with Mathlib's `Nat.succ_mul` -/
@[simp] theorem succ_mul (n m: Nat) : (n++) * m = n * m + m :=
  recurse_succ (fun _ prod ↦ prod+m) _ _

@[simp] theorem one_mul' (m: Nat) : 1 * m = 0 + m := by
  rw [←zero_succ, succ_mul, zero_mul]

/-- Compare with Mathlib's `Nat.one_mul` -/

  rw [one_mul', zero_add]

@[simp] theorem two_mul (m: Nat) : 2 * m = 0 + m + m := by
  rw [←one_succ, succ_mul, one_mul']

/- The ability to work in multiple universe is not relevant immediately, but
becomes relevant when constructing models of set theory in the Chapter 3 epilogue. -/
universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Enable `X ∪ Y` for `X Y : Set`. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := union_pair

/-- Enable `X ⊆ Y` for `X Y : Set`. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x : Object, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

/-- Exercise 3.1.7 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.subset_union_right (A B : Set) : B ⊆ A ∪ B := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

/--

  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.

  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.

  coe A := A.toSubtype

  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq n := if 0 ≤ n then a n.toNat else 0
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    have hneg : ¬ 0 ≤ n := by exact not_le.mpr hn
    simpa [hneg]

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

/-- Start a sequence from `n₁` (values before `max a.n₀ n₁` are zero). -/
def Sequence.from (a : Sequence) (n₁ : ℤ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := max a.n₀ n₁
  seq n := if n ≥ max a.n₀ n₁ then a n else 0
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    have : ¬ n ≥ max a.n₀ n₁ := not_le.mpr hn
    simpa [this]

/-- Bounded-by predicate for sequences. -/
abbrev Sequence.BoundedBy (a : Sequence) (M : ℚ) : Prop := ∀ n, |a n| ≤ M

/-- A sequence is bounded if it is bounded by some nonnegative `M`. -/
abbrev Sequence.IsBounded (a : Sequence) : Prop := ∃ M ≥ 0, a.BoundedBy M

end Chapter5

/-- ε-closeness for rationals. -/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y : ℚ) : Prop := |x - y| ≤ ε

/-- ε-close sequences from an index onwards (relative to their starts). -/
abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε : ℚ) (a b : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → Rat.Close ε (a n) (b n)

/-- Eventually ε-close sequences (after some index `N`). -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε : ℚ) (a b : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, Rat.CloseSeq ε (a.from N) (b.from N)

/--
A real-valued sequence indexed by all integers, with a starting index `m` such that
the sequence is zero to the left of `m`.
-/
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Allow writing `a n` for `a : Sequence`. -/
instance : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ => ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe s := s.seq

/-- Build a `Sequence` from a function `ℕ → ℝ`, extended by zero to the left of 0. -/
@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
  { m := 0
    seq := fun n => if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by
      intro n hn
      have : ¬ n ≥ 0 := not_le.mpr hn
      simp [this] }

/-- Coerce a function `ℕ → ℝ` to a `Sequence` (starting at 0). -/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

/-- `a` tends to `L` if it eventually stays within `ε` of `L` for every `ε > 0`.
We quantify over integer indices, respecting the sequence's starting index `m`.
-/
def Sequence.TendsTo (a : Sequence) (L : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0 : ℝ), ∃ N : ℤ, ∀ n : ℤ, n ≥ max a.m N → |a n - L| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter6

/-- A sequence of real numbers indexed by integers, with a starting index `m` after which the
values are given by `seq`, and before which they vanish. -/
structure Sequence where
  m   : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Allow using a sequence as a function `ℤ → ℝ`. -/
instance : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ => ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

/-- Build a sequence from a function on the tail `{n // n ≥ m}` by extending with zeros before `m`. -/
abbrev Sequence.mk' (m : ℤ) (a : { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence
    where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

/-- The tail of a sequence starting from index `m₁`. -/
abbrev Sequence.«from» (a : Sequence) (m₁ : ℤ) : Sequence :=
  Sequence.mk' (max a.m m₁) (fun n => a n)

/-- Bounded-above-by predicate for a sequence. -/
abbrev Sequence.BddAboveBy (a : Sequence) (M : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.m, a n ≤ M

/-- Bounded above predicate for a sequence. -/
abbrev Sequence.BddAbove (a : Sequence) : Prop := ∃ M, a.BddAboveBy M

/-- Bounded-below-by predicate for a sequence. -/
abbrev Sequence.BddBelowBy (a : Sequence) (M : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.m, a n ≥ M

/-- Bounded below predicate for a sequence. -/
abbrev Sequence.BddBelow (a : Sequence) : Prop := ∃ M, a.BddBelowBy M

namespace Real

/-- Closeness at scale `ε`: two reals are `ε`-close if their distance is at most `ε`. -/
abbrev Close (ε : ℝ) (x y : ℝ) : Prop := |x - y| ≤ ε

/-- `x` is `ε`-adherent to the sequence `a` if some tail term is `ε`-close to `x`. -/
abbrev Adherent (ε : ℝ) (a : Chapter6.Sequence) (x : ℝ) :=
  ∃ n ≥ a.m, Close ε (a n) x

/-- `x` is continually adherent to `a` at scale `ε` if every tail contains a term `ε`-close to `x`. -/
abbrev ContinuallyAdherent (ε : ℝ) (a : Chapter6.Sequence) (x : ℝ) :=
  ∀ N ≥ a.m, Adherent ε (a.«from» N) x

end Real

/-- Limit point of a sequence (in ℝ). -/
abbrev Sequence.LimitPoint (a : Sequence) (x : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0 : ℝ), (Real.ContinuallyAdherent ε a x)

/-- The (extended-real) supremum of the image of a sequence. -/
noncomputable abbrev Sequence.sup (a : Sequence) : EReal :=
  sSup {x : EReal | ∃ n ≥ a.m, x = (a n)}

/-- The upper sequence `N ↦ sup (a.from N)`. -/
noncomputable abbrev Sequence.upperseq (a : Sequence) : ℤ → EReal := fun N ↦ (a.«from» N).sup

/-- The limsup as an extended real: `inf` of the upper sequence values. -/
noncomputable abbrev Sequence.limsup (a : Sequence) : EReal :=
  sInf {x : EReal | ∃ N ≥ a.m, x = a.upperseq N}

/-- Extended limit point predicate. -/
abbrev Sequence.ExtendedLimitPoint (a : Sequence) (x : EReal) : Prop :=
  if hx : x = ⊤ then ¬ a.BddAbove
  else if hx' : x = ⊥ then ¬ a.BddBelow
  else a.LimitPoint (EReal.toReal x)

/-- Exercise 6.4.8: every limsup is an extended limit point. -/
 theorem Sequence.extended_limit_point_of_limsup (a:Sequence) : a.ExtendedLimitPoint a.limsup := by
  sorry

end Chapter6

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem :
    Object →
      Set →
        Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset : Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x :
    mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔
      (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop) (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop) (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') :
    ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv :
    ℕ ≃
      Subtype
        (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) : ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X : Set) (Y : Set) : (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X : Set) (Y : Set) (F : Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f : Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X), function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

namespace SetTheory
instance objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where mem X x := mem x X
namespace Set
instance instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where emptyCollection := emptyset
instance instSubset : HasSubset Set where Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y
instance instSSubset : HasSSubset Set where SSubset X Y := X ⊆ Y ∧ X ≠ Y
instance instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where singleton := singleton
end Set
end SetTheory

/-- Allow `x ∈ X` with `x : Object` and `X : Set`. -/

  mem X x := mem x X

@[ext]
structure Function (X Y : Set) where
  P : X → Y → Prop
  unique : ∀ x : X, ∃! y : Y, P x y

namespace Function

/-- Build a Chapter 3 function from a Mathlib function. -/
abbrev mk_fn {X Y : Set} (f : X → Y) : Function X Y :=
  Function.mk (fun x y ↦ y = f x) (by simp)

/-- Convert a Chapter 3 function to a Mathlib function (using choice). -/
noncomputable def to_fn {X Y : Set} (f : Function X Y) : X → Y :=
  fun x ↦ Classical.choose (ExistsUnique.exists (f.unique x))

/-- Coerce a Chapter 3 function to a Mathlib function. -/
noncomputable instance instCoefn (X Y : Set) : CoeFun (Function X Y) (fun _ ↦ X → Y) where
  coe := Function.to_fn

/-- Composition of Chapter 3 functions. -/
noncomputable abbrev comp {X Y Z : Set} (g : Function Y Z) (f : Function X Y) : Function X Z :=
  Function.mk_fn (fun x ↦ g (f x))

-- Use ○ for Chapter 3 function composition to avoid conflict with Mathlib's ∘.
infix:90 "○" => Function.comp

/-- One-to-one (injective) Chapter 3 function. -/
abbrev one_to_one {X Y : Set} (f : Function X Y) : Prop :=
  ∀ x x' : X, x ≠ x' → f x ≠ f x'

end Function

open Function

theorem Function.comp_of_inj {X Y Z:Set} {f: Function X Y} {g : Function Y Z} (hf: f.one_to_one)
  (hg: g.one_to_one) : (g ○ f).one_to_one := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

namespace Rat
/-- Two sequences `a` and `b` are eventually within `ε` of each other if there exists
an index after which all terms are within `ε` in absolute difference. -/
def EventuallyClose (ε : ℚ) (a b : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N : ℤ, ∀ n : ℤ, n ≥ N → |a n - b n| ≤ ε
end Rat

abbrev Equiv (a b : ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0 : ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a : Sequence) (b : Sequence)

abbrev BoundedBy (a : Sequence) (M : ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, |a n| ≤ M

abbrev IsBounded (a : Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ M ≥ 0, a.BoundedBy M

end Sequence

/-- Definition 6.1.1 (Distance/ε-close). -/
abbrev Real.Close (ε x y : ℝ) : Prop := dist x y ≤ ε

/-- Unfolding lemma for Real.Close. -/
theorem Real.close_def (ε x y : ℝ) : ε.Close x y ↔ dist x y ≤ ε := by rfl

namespace Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.3 (Sequence). -/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

/-- Alternative constructor for sequences starting from a given index. -/
abbrev Sequence.mk' (m:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

/-- Shift a sequence to start from m₁. -/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (m₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.m m₁) (a ↑·)

end Chapter6

/-- Adherence at scale ε. -/
abbrev Real.Adherent (ε:ℝ) (a:Chapter6.Sequence) (x:ℝ) := ∃ n ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) x

/-- Continual adherence at scale ε. -/
abbrev Real.ContinuallyAdherent (ε:ℝ) (a:Chapter6.Sequence) (x:ℝ) :=
  ∀ N ≥ a.m, ε.Adherent (a.from N) x

namespace Chapter6

open EReal

/-- Limit point of a real sequence (in ℝ). -/
abbrev Sequence.LimitPoint (a:Sequence) (x:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.ContinuallyAdherent a x

/-- Bounded-above and below notions for sequences. -/
abbrev Sequence.BddAboveBy (a : Sequence) (M : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.m, a n ≤ M

abbrev Sequence.BddAbove (a : Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ M, a.BddAboveBy M

abbrev Sequence.BddBelowBy (a : Sequence) (M : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.m, a n ≥ M

abbrev Sequence.BddBelow (a : Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ M, a.BddBelowBy M

/-- Infimum of the values of a sequence (in EReal). -/
noncomputable abbrev Sequence.inf (a : Sequence) : EReal :=
  sInf {x | ∃ n ≥ a.m, x = a n}

/-- Lower sequence: infima of tails. -/
noncomputable abbrev Sequence.lowerseq (a : Sequence) : ℤ → EReal := fun N ↦ (a.from N).inf

/-- Liminf (in EReal). -/
noncomputable abbrev Sequence.liminf (a : Sequence) : EReal :=
  sSup {x | ∃ N ≥ a.m, x = a.lowerseq N}

/-- Extended limit point notion (in EReal). -/
abbrev Sequence.ExtendedLimitPoint (a : Sequence) (x : EReal) : Prop :=
  if x = ⊤ then ¬ a.BddAbove else if x = ⊥ then ¬ a.BddBelow else a.LimitPoint x.toReal

/-- Exercise 6.4.8 -/
theorem Sequence.extended_limit_point_of_liminf (a:Sequence) : a.ExtendedLimitPoint a.liminf := by
  sorry

end Chapter6

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Universal specification) -/
abbrev axiom_of_universal_specification : Prop :=
  ∀ P : Object → Prop, ∃ A : Set, ∀ x : Object, x ∈ A ↔ P x

/--
  Exercise 3.2.1. The spirit of the exercise is to establish these results without using either
  Russell's paradox, or the union operation.
-/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.union_exists (h: axiom_of_universal_specification) (A B:Set):
    ∃ (Z:Set), ∀ z, z ∈ Z ↔ z ∈ A ∨ z ∈ B := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

abbrev Real.Close (ε x y : ℝ) : Prop := dist x y ≤ ε

namespace Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.3 (Sequence). -/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a:ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
 {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by simp_all
 }

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as sequences. -/
instance Sequence.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (m:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) (h: n ≥ m) :
    (Sequence.mk' m a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℝ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by simp

/-- Start a sequence from a later index. -/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (m₁:ℤ) : Sequence := mk' (max a.m m₁) (a ↑·)

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {m₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ m₁) :
  (a.from m₁) n = a n := by
  simp [hn]; intros; symm; solve_by_elim [a.vanish]

end Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/ 
abbrev Real.CloseSeq (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop := ∀ n ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) L

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/ 
abbrev Real.EventuallyClose (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.m, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L

namespace Chapter6

/-- Limits of sequences. -/
abbrev Sequence.TendsTo (a:Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L

/-- Convergent sequences. -/
abbrev Sequence.Convergent (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∃ L, a.TendsTo L

/-- Divergent sequences. -/
abbrev Sequence.Divergent (a:Sequence) : Prop := ¬ a.Convergent

    (fun (n:ℕ) ↦ ((n+1:ℝ)^(q:ℝ)):Sequence).Divergent := by

end Chapter6

export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq_iff (X Y:Set) : (X: Object) = (Y: Object) ↔  X = Y := by
  constructor
  · intro h; exact SetTheory.set_to_object.inj' h
  · rintro rfl; rfl

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

parameters not present in the output type `Output` -/

  coe := SetTheory.Set.coe_of_fun

theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_of_fun_inj {X Y:Set} (f g:X → Y) : (f:Object) = (g:Object) ↔ f = g := by
  simp [SetTheory.Set.coe_of_fun]

    ∃ Z:Set, ∀ F:Object, F ∈ Z ↔ ∃ X' Y':Set, X' ⊆ X ∧ Y' ⊆ Y ∧ ∃ f: X' → Y', F = f := by

  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

noncomputable abbrev right_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ := lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Ioi x₀)).map f)

noncomputable abbrev left_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ := lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Iio x₀)).map f)

noncomputable abbrev α_length (α: ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
| BoundedInterval.Icc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
| BoundedInterval.Ico a b => if a ≤ b then (left_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
| BoundedInterval.Ioc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0
| BoundedInterval.Ioo a b => if a < b then (left_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0

notation3:max α"["I"]ₗ" => α_length α I

namespace PiecewiseConstantWith

noncomputable abbrev RS_integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) (α: ℝ → ℝ)   :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * α[J]ₗ

  sorry

end PiecewiseConstantWith

    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11

  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := Set.toSubtype A

@[ext]
structure Function (X Y : Set) where
  P : X → Y → Prop
  unique : ∀ x : X, ∃! y : Y, P x y

noncomputable def Function.to_fn {X Y : Set} (f : Function X Y) : X → Y :=
  fun x ↦ (f.unique x).choose

noncomputable instance Function.inst_coefn (X Y : Set) :
    CoeFun (Function X Y) (fun _ ↦ X → Y) where
  coe := Function.to_fn

@[simp] theorem Function.to_fn_eval {X Y : Set} (f : Function X Y) (x : X) :
    f.to_fn x = f x := rfl

abbrev Function.one_to_one {X Y : Set} (f : Function X Y) : Prop :=
  ∀ x x' : X, x ≠ x' → f x ≠ f x'

abbrev Function.onto {X Y : Set} (f : Function X Y) : Prop :=
  ∀ y : Y, ∃ x : X, f x = y

abbrev Function.bijective {X Y : Set} (f : Function X Y) : Prop :=
  f.one_to_one ∧ f.onto

/-- Compatibility lemma with Mathlib's `Function.Injective` style -/
theorem Function.one_to_one_iff {X Y : Set} (f : Function X Y) :
    f.one_to_one ↔ ∀ x x' : X, f x = f x' → x = x' := by
  constructor
  · intro hinj x x' hfx
    by_contra hneq
    exact (hinj x x' hneq) hfx
  · intro h x x' hneq hfeq
    exact hneq (h x x' hfeq)

open Classical in
abbrev Function.inverse {X Y : Set} (f : Function X Y) (h : f.bijective) :
    Function Y X :=
  Function.mk (fun y x ↦ f x = y) (by
    intros
    apply existsUnique_of_exists_of_unique
    . aesop
    intro _ _ hx hx'; simp at hx hx'
    rw [←hx'] at hx
    apply f.one_to_one_iff.mp h.1
    simp [hx]
  )

theorem Function.inverse_comp_self {X Y: Set} {f: Function X Y} (h: f.bijective) (x: X) :
    (f.inverse h) (f x) = x := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as series. -/

  coe := fun a ↦ {

    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind
  }

    (Series.mk' a).seq n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

/-- Definition 7.2.2 (Convergence of series) -/
abbrev Series.partial (s : Series) (N:ℤ) : ℝ := ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

theorem Series.partial_succ (s : Series) {N:ℤ} (h: N ≥ s.m-1) : s.partial (N+1) = s.partial N + s.seq (N+1) := by
  unfold Series.partial
  rw [add_comm (s.partial N) _]
  convert Finset.sum_insert (show N+1 ∉ Finset.Icc s.m N by simp)
  symm; apply Finset.insert_Icc_right_eq_Icc_add_one; linarith

theorem Series.partial_of_lt {s : Series} {N:ℤ} (h: N < s.m) : s.partial N = 0 := by
  unfold Series.partial
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero]
  intro n hn; simp at hn; grind

abbrev Series.convergesTo (s : Series) (L:ℝ) : Prop := Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

abbrev Series.converges (s : Series) : Prop := ∃ L, s.convergesTo L

abbrev Series.diverges (s : Series) : Prop := ¬s.converges

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev Series.sum (s : Series) : ℝ := if h : s.converges then h.choose else 0

theorem Series.converges_of_convergesTo {s : Series} {L:ℝ} (h: s.convergesTo L) :
    s.converges := by use L

/-- Remark 7.2.3 -/
theorem Series.sum_of_converges {s : Series} {L:ℝ} (h: s.convergesTo L) : s.sum = L := by
  simp [sum, converges_of_convergesTo h]
  exact tendsto_nhds_unique ((converges_of_convergesTo h).choose_spec) h

theorem Series.convergesTo_uniq {s : Series} {L L':ℝ} (h: s.convergesTo L) (h': s.convergesTo L') :
    L = L' := tendsto_nhds_unique h h'

theorem Series.convergesTo_sum {s : Series} (h: s.converges) : s.convergesTo s.sum := by
  simp [sum, h]; exact h.choose_spec

abbrev Series.nonneg (s: Series) : Prop := ∀ n, s.seq n ≥ 0

  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A 
      ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

instance SetTheory.Set.instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

/-- Definition 3.4.1.  Interestingly, the definition does not require S to be a subset of X. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.image {X Y:Set} (f:X → Y) (S: Set) : Set :=
  X.replace (P := fun x y ↦ f x = y ∧ x.val ∈ S) (by
    intro x y y' h
    rcases h with ⟨⟨hxy, hxS⟩, ⟨hxy', hxS'⟩⟩
    exact (by simpa [hxy] using hxy')
  )

/-- Definition 3.4.4 (inverse images). Again, it is not required that U be a subset of Y. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.preimage {X Y:Set} (f:X → Y) (U: Set) : Set := X.specify (P := fun x ↦ (f x).val ∈ U)

/-- Exercise 3.4.5 -/ 
 theorem SetTheory.Set.image_preimage_of_surj {X Y:Set} (f:X → Y) :
    (∀ S, S ⊆ Y → image f (preimage f S) = S) ↔ Function.Surjective f := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

abbrev Constant {X Y : Type} (f : X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

abbrev ConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : Prop :=

abbrev MajorizesOn (g f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∀ x ∈ (I : Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∀ x ∈ (I : Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

-- Partitions of a bounded interval
@[ext]
structure Partition (I : BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x : ℝ) (hx : x ∈ (I : Set ℝ)) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ (J : Set ℝ)
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : (J : Set ℝ) ⊆ (I : Set ℝ)

instance instMembership (I : BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f : ℝ → ℝ) {I : BoundedInterval} (P : Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J : Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

-- Constant value selectors
open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y : Type} [hY : Nonempty Y] (f : X → Y) : Y :=
  if h : Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : ℝ :=
  constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

-- One-sided limits and α-length
noncomputable abbrev right_lim (f : ℝ → ℝ) (x₀ : ℝ) : ℝ :=
  lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Ioi x₀)).map f)

noncomputable abbrev left_lim (f : ℝ → ℝ) (x₀ : ℝ) : ℝ :=
  lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Iio x₀)).map f)

noncomputable abbrev α_length (α : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  match I with
  | BoundedInterval.Icc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
  | BoundedInterval.Ico a b => if a ≤ b then (left_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
  | BoundedInterval.Ioc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0
  | BoundedInterval.Ioo a b => if a < b then (left_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0

notation3:max α "[" I "]ₗ" => α_length α I

  ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J : Set ℝ) * α[J]ₗ

noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval)

noncomputable abbrev lower_RS_integral (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) (α : ℝ → ℝ) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ · I α) ''
    {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

abbrev BddOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

end Chapter9

noncomputable abbrev RS_IntegrableOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) (α : ℝ → ℝ) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_RS_integral f I α = upper_RS_integral f I α

/-- Intervals with endpoints a,b and one of the four openness/closedness combinations. -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b : ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b : ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b : ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b : ℝ) : BoundedInterval

namespace BoundedInterval

/-- Left endpoint of a bounded interval. -/
abbrev a (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

/-- Right endpoint of a bounded interval. -/
abbrev b (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

/-- Length of a bounded interval. -/
abbrev length (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Coercion to the corresponding set. -/
@[coe]
def toSet (I : BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ :=
  match I with
  | Ioo a b => Set.Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => Set.Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => Set.Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => Set.Ico a b

instance instCoe : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) := ⟨toSet⟩

/-- Membership of a real in a bounded interval means membership in the corresponding set. -/
instance instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I : Set ℝ)

end BoundedInterval

notation "|" I "|ₗ" => BoundedInterval.length I

/-- A constant function (on any type). -/
abbrev Constant {X Y : Type} (f : X → Y) : Prop :=
  ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

/-- Constant on a set of reals. -/
abbrev ConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : Prop :=
  Constant (fun x : X ↦ f x)

/-- Majorization on a bounded interval. -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∀ x ∈ (I : Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

/-- A partition of a bounded interval into finitely many bounded intervals. -/
@[ext]
structure Partition (I : BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x : ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : (J : Set ℝ) ⊆ (I : Set ℝ)

namespace Partition

instance instMembership (I : BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

end Partition

/-- Piecewise constant with respect to a given partition. -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f : ℝ → ℝ) {I : BoundedInterval} (P : Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J : Set ℝ)

/-- Piecewise constant on some partition of a bounded interval. -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

open Classical in
/-- Choose a canonical value of a constant function. -/
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y : Type} [hY : Nonempty Y] (f : X → Y) : Y :=
  if h : Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

/-- Choose a canonical value of a function on a set. -/
noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : ℝ :=
  constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f x)

open scoped BigOperators

namespace PiecewiseConstantWith

/-- Integral of a piecewise-constant function with respect to a fixed partition. -/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f : ℝ → ℝ) {I : BoundedInterval} (P : Partition I) : ℝ :=
  ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J : Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

end PiecewiseConstantWith

namespace PiecewiseConstantOn

open Classical in
/-- Integral of a piecewise-constant function on a bounded interval. -/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  if h : PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

end PiecewiseConstantOn

/-- Upper integral as the infimum over piecewise-constant majorants. -/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- We define integ to be the upper integral (as in the source). -/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  upper_integral f I

  ∀ x ∈ Set.Icc a b, f x = 0 := by

/-- ZF-style set theory with atoms, as in Chapter 3.1. -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality (X Y : Set) : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset : Set

  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom (x y : Object) : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom (X Y : Set) (x : Object) : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem · A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem · A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem · A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem · A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem · nat)
  regularity_axiom (A : Set) (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X Y : Set) : (Subtype (mem · X) → Subtype (mem · Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X Y : Set) (F : Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f : Subtype (mem · Y) → Subtype (mem · X), function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom (A : Set) (x : Object) :
    mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Objects are elements of sets. -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Sets are treated as objects when needed. -/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Extensionality for sets. -/
@[ext]
theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y : Set} (h : ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

/-- Coerce a set to its subtype of elements so we can treat sets as types. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

/-- Notation ∅ for the empty set. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

/-- Notation {x} for singletons. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

/-- Use ∪ for pairwise union. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

namespace SetTheory
namespace Set

/-- Subset notation between Chapter 3 sets. -/
instance instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

/-- A convenient abbreviation for specification using the coercion to Sort. -/
abbrev specify (A : Set) (P : A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

/-- Finite cardinals inside our framework. -/
abbrev Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  SetTheory.nat.specify (fun m : Subtype (SetTheory.mem · SetTheory.nat) ↦ (SetTheory.nat_equiv.symm m) < n)

/-- Equal cardinality (Definition 3.6.1). -/
abbrev EqualCard (X Y : Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

/-- `≈` on sets is equal-cardinality. -/
instance EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid Set := {
  r := EqualCard,
  iseqv := by
    refine ⟨?refl, ?symm, ?trans⟩
    · intro X; exact ⟨id, Function.bijective_id⟩
    · intro X Y h; rcases h with ⟨f, hf⟩
      refine ⟨(Equiv.ofBijective f hf).symm, ?_⟩
      exact (Equiv.ofBijective f hf).symm.bijective
    · intro X Y Z hXY hYZ
      rcases hXY with ⟨f, hf⟩
      rcases hYZ with ⟨g, hg⟩
      exact ⟨g ∘ f, hg.comp hf⟩
}

/-- Definition 3.6.5: `X` has cardinality `n` if `X ≈ Fin n`. -/
abbrev has_card (X : Set) (n : ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

end Set
end SetTheory

namespace Set

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Example 3.6.7 -/
 theorem Example_3_6_7a (a : Object) : ({a} : Set).has_card 1 := by
  sorry

end Set
end SetTheory

end Chapter3

  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5

  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :

    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

import Mathlib.Tactic

/--
  This definition needs to be made outside of the Section 4.3 namespace for technical reasons.
-/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). To avoid some technicalities involving dependent types, we extend
  sequences by zero to the left of the starting point `n₀`.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    have : ¬ n ≥ n₀ := by exact not_le.mpr hn
    simp [this]

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

namespace Sequence

theorem IsCauchy_iff (a:Chapter5.Sequence) :
    a.IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ∀ n ≥ N, ∀ m ≥ N, |a n - a m| ≤ ε := by
  sorry

end Sequence
end Chapter5

@[simp] theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq_iff (X Y : Set) : (X : Object) = (Y : Object) ↔ X = Y :=
  ⟨ fun h => SetTheory.set_to_object.inj' h, by rintro rfl; rfl ⟩

abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x => x ∈ A)

-- We'll need Nat as a Set; give a name to it

def nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

-- For Fin, we need specify; define an abbreviation as in Section_3_6
abbrev specify (A : Set) (P : A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

-- Finite analog within Set theory
abbrev Fin (n : ℕ) : Set := nat.specify (fun m : Nat => (nat_equiv.symm m) < n)

-- EqualCard relation
abbrev EqualCard (X Y : Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem EqualCard.refl (X : Set) : EqualCard X X :=
by
  refine ⟨id, ?_⟩
  exact Function.bijective_id

@[symm]
theorem EqualCard.symm {X Y : Set} (h : EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X :=
by
  rcases h with ⟨f, hf⟩
  let e := Equiv.ofBijective f hf
  exact ⟨e.symm, e.symm.bijective⟩

@[trans]
theorem EqualCard.trans {X Y Z : Set} (h1 : EqualCard X Y) (h2 : EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z :=
by
  rcases h1 with ⟨f, hf⟩
  rcases h2 with ⟨g, hg⟩
  exact ⟨ (fun x => g (f x)), hg.comp hf ⟩

instance EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid Set := ⟨ EqualCard, ⟨ EqualCard.refl, EqualCard.symm, EqualCard.trans ⟩ ⟩

-- has_card, finite, and card
abbrev has_card (X : Set) (n : ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

abbrev finite (X : Set) : Prop := ∃ n : ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
noncomputable def card (X : Set) : ℕ := if h : X.finite then h.choose else 0

end SetTheory.Set

namespace SetTheory.Set

/-- Coerce a set to the subtype of objects it contains, so one can write `x : X`. -/
abbrev toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

/-- Allow using a set as a type of its elements. -/
instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := toSubtype A

  coe n := nat_equiv.symm n

/-- Finite "set of naturals below n" inside Chapter 3 set theory. -/
abbrev Fin (n : ℕ) : Set := (Nat).specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

/-- Provide the setoid so we can use the notation `≈` for equal cardinality. -/
instance EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid Set := by
  classical
  refine ⟨EqualCard, ?_⟩
  refine ⟨?refl, ?symm, ?trans⟩
  · intro X; refine ⟨id, ?_⟩; simpa using Function.bijective_id
  · intro X Y h; rcases h with ⟨f, hf⟩
    let e : X ≃ Y := Equiv.ofBijective f hf
    exact ⟨e.symm, e.symm.bijective⟩
  · intro X Y Z hXY hYZ
    rcases hXY with ⟨f, hf⟩; rcases hYZ with ⟨g, hg⟩
    refine ⟨(fun x => g (f x)), ?_⟩
    simpa [Function.comp] using hg.comp hf

/-- Definition 3.6.5: a set has cardinality `n` if it is equivalent to `Fin n`. -/
abbrev has_card (X : Set) (n : ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

end SetTheory.Set

  sorry

  vanish := by simp_all }

  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.

-- Provide membership notation x ∈ X for Object x and Set X
instance objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where

namespace Set

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

/-- Replacement and specification (restate using the SetTheory structure) -/

abbrev specify (A : Set) (P : A → Prop) : Set :=
  SetTheory.specify A P

-- Define subset and strict subset relations on sets

  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

abbrev preimage {X Y : Set} (f : X → Y) (U : Set) : Set := X.specify (P := fun x ↦ (f x).val ∈ U)

end Set
end SetTheory

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u

  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object

  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset : Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset

  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x

  nat : Set

    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S

    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f : Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
      function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Allow writing `x ∈ X` for `Object` in `Set` (from Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Sets coerce to objects (from Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- If two sets are equal as objects, they are equal as sets (from Section 3.1). -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq {X Y : Set} (h : (X : Object) = (Y : Object)) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.set_to_object.inj' h

@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq_iff (X Y : Set) : (X : Object) = (Y : Object) ↔ X = Y :=
  ⟨ SetTheory.Set.coe_eq, by rintro rfl; rfl ⟩

/-- Extensionality for sets (from Section 3.1). -/
@[ext]
theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y : Set} (h : ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

/-- Turn a set into a subtype of objects (from Section 3.1). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

/-- Treat sets as types via their underlying subtype of objects (from Section 3.1). -/
instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

/-- Empty set notation for our sets (from Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

/-- Singletons (from Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.mem_singleton (x a : Object) : x ∈ ({a} : Set) ↔ x = a :=
  SetTheory.singleton_axiom x a

/-- Pairwise union notation for our sets (from Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.mem_union (x : Object) (X Y : Set) : x ∈ (X ∪ Y) ↔ (x ∈ X ∨ x ∈ Y) :=
  SetTheory.union_pair_axiom X Y x

/-- Specification as a convenience abbreviation (from Section 3.1). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A : Set) (P : A → Prop) : Set :=
  SetTheory.specify A P

/-- Intersection notation for our sets (from Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instIntersection : Inter Set where
  inter X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∈ Y)

-- Now we have `∩` notation available for our sets.

/-- A copy of the set of naturals inside the set theory (from Section 3.1). -/
abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Equivalence between `ℕ` and the set-theoretic naturals (from Section 3.1). -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

/-- Coercion from set-theoretic naturals to `ℕ` (from Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv.symm n

/-- A `Fin`-like set with `n` elements inside the set theory (as in Sections 3.5/3.6). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  Nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

/-- Equal cardinality between two sets (Definition 3.6.1). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y : Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

namespace SetTheory.Set

@[refl]
theorem EqualCard.refl (X : Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  exact ⟨id, Function.bijective_id⟩

@[symm]
theorem EqualCard.symm {X Y : Set} (h : EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  classical
  rcases h with ⟨f, hf⟩
  let e := Equiv.ofBijective f hf
  -- use the inverse equivalence as the bijection in the opposite direction
  exact ⟨e.symm, Equiv.bijective _⟩

@[trans]
theorem EqualCard.trans {X Y Z : Set} (h1 : EqualCard X Y) (h2 : EqualCard Y Z) :
    EqualCard X Z := by
  rcases h1 with ⟨f, hf⟩
  rcases h2 with ⟨g, hg⟩
  exact ⟨g ∘ f, hg.comp hf⟩

/-- The setoid corresponding to equal cardinality (Proposition 3.6.4). -/
instance EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

/-- A set has cardinality `n` if it is equivalent to `Fin n` (Definition 3.6.5). -/
abbrev has_card (X : Set) (n : ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

/-- Finite sets (Section 3.6). -/
abbrev finite (X : Set) : Prop := ∃ n : ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
/-- Define the cardinality of a set; infinite sets get the junk cardinality `0`. -/
noncomputable def card (X : Set) : ℕ := if h : X.finite then h.choose else 0

end SetTheory.Set

/-- Finally, the target theorem from Section 3.6. -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.card_union_add_card_inter {A B : Set} (hA : A.finite) (hB : B.finite) :
    A.card + B.card = (A ∪ B).card + (A ∩ B).card := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

  | Icc a b => .Icc a b

  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

namespace SetTheory

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/

  mem X x := mem x X

namespace SetTheory.Set

/-- Coerce a `Set` to a subtype of `Object` so we can treat it as a type. -/
abbrev toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := toSubtype A

/-- Subset relation on sets. -/
instance instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

/-- Use replacement from the axioms. -/

end SetTheory.Set

namespace SetTheory.Set

/-- Equal cardinality (Definition 3.6.1). -/
abbrev EqualCard (X Y : Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem EqualCard.refl (X : Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  exact ⟨id, Function.bijective_id⟩

@[symm]
theorem EqualCard.symm {X Y : Set} (h : EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  rcases h with ⟨f, hf⟩
  refine ⟨(Equiv.ofBijective f hf).symm, ?_⟩
  exact (Equiv.ofBijective f hf).symm.bijective

@[trans]
theorem EqualCard.trans {X Y Z : Set} (h1 : EqualCard X Y) (h2 : EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  rcases h1 with ⟨f, hf⟩
  rcases h2 with ⟨g, hg⟩
  exact ⟨g ∘ f, (hg.comp hf)⟩

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1 -/
instance EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set :=
  ⟨ EqualCard, {refl := EqualCard.refl, symm := EqualCard.symm, trans := EqualCard.trans} ⟩

/-- Image of a set under a function (using replacement). -/
abbrev image {X Y : Set} (f : X → Y) (S : Set) : Set :=
  X.replace (P := fun x y ↦ f x = y ∧ x.val ∈ S) (by
    intro x y y' h
    rcases h with ⟨h1, h2⟩
    rcases h1 with ⟨hfy, _⟩
    rcases h2 with ⟨hfy', _⟩
    simpa [hfy] using hfy'
  )

/-- A set of naturals up to `n` (our in-chapter `Fin n`). -/
abbrev nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

abbrev Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  nat.specify (fun m ↦ (SetTheory.nat_equiv.symm m) < n)

/-- Cardinality `n`. -/
abbrev has_card (X : Set) (n : ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

/-- Finiteness. -/
abbrev finite (X : Set) : Prop := ∃ n : ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
/-- A convenient junk cardinality for infinite sets. -/
noncomputable def card (X : Set) : ℕ := if h : X.finite then h.choose else 0

end SetTheory.Set

open SetTheory.Set

/-- Exercise 3.6.11 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.two_to_two_iff {X Y:Set} (f: X → Y): Function.Injective f ↔
    ∀ S ⊆ X, S.card = 2 → (image f S).card = 2 := by
  sorry

  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) 
      ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : 
      ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P
abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv.symm n

/-- Definition of `Fin n` as a Chapter 3 set of naturals less than `n`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

/-- Definition 3.6.1 (Equal cardinality) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: EqualCard X Y) (h2: EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1 -/
instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

/-- Definition 3.6.5 -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ SetTheory.Set.Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
/-- It is convenient for Lean purposes to give infinite sets the ``junk`` cardinality of zero. -/
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

/-- Indexed union -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.iUnion (I: Set) (A: I → Set) : Set :=
  SetTheory.union (I.replace (P := fun α S ↦ S = A α) (by grind))

/-- Exercise 3.6.10 -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.pigeonhole_principle {n:ℕ} {A: Fin n → Set}
  (hA: ∀ i, (A i).finite) (hAcard: (iUnion _ A).card > n) : ∃ i, (A i).card ≥ 2 := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

@[ext]
structure Sequence where

  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
  vanish := by
    grind

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by simp [hn]; intro h; exact (a.vanish _ h).symm

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

lemma Rat.steady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.Steady a ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m) := by rfl

abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

lemma Rat.eventuallySteady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.EventuallySteady a ↔ ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N) := by rfl

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

lemma Sequence.isCauchy_def (a:Sequence) :
  a.IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a := by rfl

/-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy -/ 
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_eq {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (mk' ha).toSequence = (a:Sequence) := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/ 
theorem Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) (hbc: Equiv b c) :
  Equiv a c := by sorry

instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := sorry
     symm := sorry
     trans := sorry
  }

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Chapter5.Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

/-- Definition 5.3.1 (Real numbers) -/ 

theorem Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  constructor
  · intro h; replace h := Quotient.exact h
    rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb] at h
  intro h; apply Quotient.sound
  rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb]

/--Lemma 5.3.6 (Sum of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy)-/ 
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/ 

theorem Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/ 

theorem Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/ 

theorem Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') :=
  Sequence.equiv_trans (add_equiv_left _ haa') (add_equiv_right _ hbb')

/-- Definition 5.3.4 (Addition of reals) -/ 
noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals
        apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_ )

/-- Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy) -/ 

theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/ 

theorem Sequence.mul_equiv
{a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
(ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
(hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
(haa': Equiv a a')
(hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') := by sorry

/-- Definition 5.3.9 (Product of reals) -/ 
noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · exact
        Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy)
          (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals
        apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_ )

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

 theorem Real.LIM_of_ge {x:Real} {a:ℕ → ℚ} (hcauchy: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (h: ∀ n, a n ≥ x) :
    LIM a ≥ x := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y

  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y

    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x

namespace Chapter11

/-- Bounded intervals used throughout Chapter 11. -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- Coercion of a bounded interval to the corresponding set. -/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

/-- Membership of a real in a bounded interval, via the coerced set. -/
instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

/-- Subset relation on bounded intervals, via the coerced sets. -/
instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

/-- Endpoints `a` and `b` of a bounded interval. -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

/-- Length of a bounded interval. -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Constant functions (global). -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Piecewise constant on a fixed partition. -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Piecewise constant on an interval. -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Integral of a piecewise-constant function with respect to a given partition. -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

/-- Integral of a piecewise-constant function on an interval. -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

open Chapter9

/-- Majorization and minorization on a bounded interval (Definition 11.3.1). -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

/-- Upper and lower Riemann integrals (Definition 11.3.2). -/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- Riemann integral as upper integral (Definition 11.3.4). -/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

/-- Riemann integrability on a bounded interval. -/
noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

open BoundedInterval

/-- The target theorem from Section 11.4. -/
 theorem IntegrableOn.split {I: BoundedInterval} {f: ℝ → ℝ} (hf: IntegrableOn f I) (P: Partition I) :
  integ f I = ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, integ f J := by
  sorry

end Chapter11
